channel.title: Shashi Budati
channel.description: Notes from the build. Usually about something that took too long to figure out.

item.title: AI Agents 101: A Glossary
item.description: Plain definitions for the terms you keep seeing — agent, loop, memory, skills — without the hype.

item.title: Don't Let AI Write Your Docs — Let It Interview Your Team
item.description: AI-generated context files hurt more than they help. The fix is using arc42 as a structure and the LLM as an interviewer.

item.title: Cheap verification is your biggest unlock when building ML products
item.description: The reason AI works where it works and fails where it fails maps almost exactly onto whether the domain has cheap verification. This is the hidden variable — not model size, not architecture.

item.title: Choose Your MLOps Platform by Team Bottleneck, Not Feature Matrix
item.description: The right MLOps platform question isn't which platform is best — it's which team function is your actual bottleneck.

item.title: Dev Tools Are Going Headless
item.description: IDE plugins are being replaced by AI skills. The tooling is following the agent.

item.title: Engineering Quality as an AI Skill
item.description: A Claude Code skill that walks a structured engineering quality checklist, generates a status report, and tracks gaps iteratively.

item.title: Your financial data model is a contract, not a convenience
item.description: Schema decisions that feel small when building a PFM or investment analytics product — and become rebuilds when you get them wrong.

item.title: Parser generators: the unglamorous tool that beats LLMs at structured parsing
item.description: For constrained-format data like SMS alerts, regex breaks down and LLMs are overkill. Parser generators give you 40x the throughput, exact outputs, and logic you can actually debug.

item.title: Stop Saving CSVs to S3
item.description: CSV is a clipboard, not a storage format. Here's why data science teams should switch to Parquet — and how row groups make large-file processing on S3 actually work.
